sections.summary: Good Status applies from CGPA 2.00 and above. Academic Probation may apply when CGPA is below 2.00. Academic Dismissal may apply if CGPA remains below 2.00 for three consecutive semesters.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for probation, dismissal, and CGPA standing questions.
sections.safety_note: Do not conclude dismissal from one CGPA value alone. Consecutive semester pattern must also be considered.

sections.response_hint: Use this section for GPA/CGPA explanation and grade-point interpretation.
sections.safety_note: If the user asks for calculation, require courses, credits, and grades.

sections.summary: Students may add courses without penalty during the first two weeks of a normal semester or the first week of a short semester. Late registration may be allowed until the fourth week of a normal semester or the second week of a short semester with approval and penalty.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for questions about changing registered courses early in the semester.
sections.safety_note: Do not extend deadlines beyond what is stated in the handbook.

sections.summary: Course withdrawal is allowed after the first two weeks but before the thirteenth week of the normal semester, or after the first week but before the seventh week of the short semester. A W grade appears on the transcript and does not count toward GPA.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for subject-level withdrawal questions.
sections.safety_note: No withdrawal is permitted after the deadline except in extraordinary circumstances.

sections.summary: Students may withdraw completely from the University before the thirteenth week of the normal semester or before the seventh week of the short semester, subject to Dean approval and required clearances.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for complete withdrawal from the University, not a single course.
sections.safety_note: Withdrawal is not official until the form and clearances are completed and returned to the Registrar’s Office.

sections.summary: Students may apply for postponement with valid reasons, subject to Dean and VP Academic approval. Postponement is not allowed after the twelfth week of the long semester or the sixth week of the short semester.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for deferment/postponement of studies.
sections.safety_note: Do not advise postponement beyond the handbook deadline.

sections.summary: Registration for new students is 2 July 2025, induction is 3-4 July 2025, first day of lecture is 7 July 2025, last day for add/drop is 18 July 2025, and final examinations run from 27 October 2025 to 9 November 2025.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for June/July 2025 intake date questions.
sections.safety_note: Important dates are semester-specific and should not be generalized to other intakes.

sections.reference: ACE305 – Bachelor of Civil Engineering with Honours
sections.summary: ACE305 is a full-time 4-year programme with 140 credits and accepts March, June/July and Sept/Oct intakes.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for Civil Engineering programme and credit questions.
sections.safety_note: Entry requirements should be quoted carefully because they are programme-specific.

sections.summary: The June/July 2025 handbook maps Software Engineering under CIT314 with a 120-credit structure.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for July intake Software Engineering questions.
sections.safety_note: Do not confuse July CIT314 with March CIT312.

sections.summary: The June/July 2025 handbook maps Computer Science under CIT313 with a 120-credit structure.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for July intake Computer Science questions.
sections.safety_note: Do not confuse July CIT313 with March CIT311.

sections.summary: The July intake structure includes CIT206 Diploma in Information Technology with 92 total credit hours.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for DIT programme questions.
sections.safety_note: Where detailed duration and credit data are needed, confirm against the programme pages.
